2921.reason: [The method may return null when the first token is not recognized by any branch, e.g., when the first token is a unary operator like '!' or a keyword like 'void' that is not handled. This can cause a NullPointerException when the caller tries to use the returned Stmt object.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The method may return null when the first token is not recognized by any branch, e.g., when the first token is a unary operator like '!' or a keyword like 'void' that is not handled. This can cause a NullPointerException when the caller tries to use the returned Stmt object.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The method may return null when the first token is BREAKTK or CONTINUETK but the next token is not SEMICN, or when the first token is IDENFR and judgeLVal() returns false but the next token is not SEMICN, or in other unhandled cases. This can cause a NullPointerException when the caller tries to use the returned Stmt object.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [This can be reached when break/continue lacks semicolon, causing null return which may lead to NPE or incorrect parsing]
